flags.concern: resize-ops: Step 1's `pendingOpsRef` plan leaks on cancelled resizes. `TimelineCanvas` only calls `onActionResizeEnd` when `cancelled === false`, so incrementing in `useClipResize.onActionResizeStart()` and decrementing only in `onActionResizeEnd()` would leave `pendingOpsRef` stuck above zero after pointer-cancel or teardown.
flags.evidence: Revised plan to address all 5 significant flags: (1) moved pendingOpsRef increment/decrement for resize to TimelineCanvas where the full session lifecycle is managed, preventing leak on cancel; (2) changed external drop protection to per-upload tracking inside each fire-and-forget IIFE; (3) dropped the undo/redo race fix — confirmed skipHistory:true on save-success means onBeforeCommit is never called, so no race exists; (4) corrected the event bus to target useTimelineCommit (not useTimelineSave) and added scheduleSaveRef as the 4th ref to replace; (5) replaced gradual compat-shim migration with explicit consumer migration step — all 3 hot consumers plus 4 light consumers are migrated to sub-context hooks in the same step.

flags.concern: external-drop: Step 2's `pendingOpsRef` finally-block is too shallow for file drops. `useExternalDrop` awaits `dispatchTimelineDrop()`, but `handleFileDrop()` returns after queuing fire-and-forget upload tasks, so a drop-level decrement would run before the async upload/replace work finishes and polling would be unblocked too early.
flags.evidence: Revised plan to address all 5 significant flags: (1) moved pendingOpsRef increment/decrement for resize to TimelineCanvas where the full session lifecycle is managed, preventing leak on cancel; (2) changed external drop protection to per-upload tracking inside each fire-and-forget IIFE; (3) dropped the undo/redo race fix — confirmed skipHistory:true on save-success means onBeforeCommit is never called, so no race exists; (4) corrected the event bus to target useTimelineCommit (not useTimelineSave) and added scheduleSaveRef as the 4th ref to replace; (5) replaced gradual compat-shim migration with explicit consumer migration step — all 3 hot consumers plus 4 light consumers are migrated to sub-context hooks in the same step.

flags.concern: timeline-history: Step 4 targets the wrong path. Save-success reconciliation already calls `commitData(..., { save: false, skipHistory: true })`, so adding an undo/redo guard inside `history.onBeforeCommit` cannot affect the stated 'save completes during undo' case and would not fix the claimed redo-stack race.
flags.evidence: Revised plan to address all 5 significant flags: (1) moved pendingOpsRef increment/decrement for resize to TimelineCanvas where the full session lifecycle is managed, preventing leak on cancel; (2) changed external drop protection to per-upload tracking inside each fire-and-forget IIFE; (3) dropped the undo/redo race fix — confirmed skipHistory:true on save-success means onBeforeCommit is never called, so no race exists; (4) corrected the event bus to target useTimelineCommit (not useTimelineSave) and added scheduleSaveRef as the 4th ref to replace; (5) replaced gradual compat-shim migration with explicit consumer migration step — all 3 hot consumers plus 4 light consumers are migrated to sub-context hooks in the same step.

flags.concern: timeline-save-bridge: The plan's ref-wiring audit is incomplete against the actual save bridge. `scheduleSaveRef` still exists and is one of the cross-hook refs called out in the brief, but the plan assumes it is gone and only replaces three refs. Step 12 also points the event emission change at `useTimelineSave`, while the live `onBeforeCommitRef` and `pruneSelectionRef` calls are in `useTimelineCommit`.
flags.evidence: Revised plan to address all 5 significant flags: (1) moved pendingOpsRef increment/decrement for resize to TimelineCanvas where the full session lifecycle is managed, preventing leak on cancel; (2) changed external drop protection to per-upload tracking inside each fire-and-forget IIFE; (3) dropped the undo/redo race fix — confirmed skipHistory:true on save-success means onBeforeCommit is never called, so no race exists; (4) corrected the event bus to target useTimelineCommit (not useTimelineSave) and added scheduleSaveRef as the 4th ref to replace; (5) replaced gradual compat-shim migration with explicit consumer migration step — all 3 hot consumers plus 4 light consumers are migrated to sub-context hooks in the same step.

flags.concern: timeline-editor-context: The gradual compat-shim strategy does not by itself deliver the promised render isolation. The hottest consumers still read the merged `useTimelineEditorContext()` surface directly, so keeping a merged hook until some later migration leaves `TimelineEditor`, `PreviewPanel`, and `PropertiesPanel` subscribed to the whole editor payload and preserves the broad re-render fan-out the plan is trying to remove.
